channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: How HIV Affects Older Adults Differently
Polypharmacy is associated with higher risk of mortality and hospitalizations among older adults
item.description: 1. Polypharmacy was associated with a higher risk of mortality and hospitalization at one-year and five-years among older adults aged >75 years. Evide...

item.title: Reasons Why Women Experience Hair Thinning

The Scan by 2 Minute Medicine®: Daily caffeine intake linked to lower dementia risk, AAP sues over federal vaccine schedule overhaul, Catherine O’Hara passes away after short illness, and GLP-1 receptor agonists reduce endometrial cancer risk
item.description: The Scan by 2 Minute Medicine® is a pop-culture medical newsletter and exclusive benefit for 2 Minute Medicine Plus subscribers. Moderate caffeine int...

item.title: Exercise Benefits for a Faster Metabolism
item.description: More intense workouts can temporarily raise your metabolic rate. Learn how exercise affects metabolism and which workouts have the greatest impact.

item.title: How Biologics Continue to Transform Dermatologic Care
item.description: Despite some remaining challenges, biologic therapy continues to revolutionize the care of people with psoriasis, atopic dermatitis, and many other co...

item.description: Before complex medical interventions ever reach patients, their effectiveness must be scientifically proven through rigorous testing in controlled set...

item.title: Q&A: What About Biosimilars in Dermatology?
item.description: The biggest misconception regarding biosimilars is that they are so different from the originator that you cannot trust them, says Steven Feldman, MD,...

item.title: More social support can sometimes lead to more—and sometimes to less—use of mental health care
item.description: People with severe mental health problems are generally thought to receive more care when they are unwell, and less care once they begin to recover. A...

item.description: Young adults with IBD are more likely to face step therapy mandates, making clinicians concerned that insurers are ignoring evidence-based guidelines ...

item.title: Reassuring Data on C-Section Infection Control
item.description: Adjunctive azithromycin had no significant impact on childhood obesity or other outcomes. Medscape Medical News
